dependencies: Dependence on third-party service providers for payment processing, payment disbursements, currency exchange, identity verification, sanctions screening, and fraud analysis
Reliance on third-party shipping providers for delivery of goods sold on platform
Dependence on third-party authentication services for item authentication
Reliance on third-party hosting providers for data center facilities
Dependence on payment card association operating rules and certification requirements
Reliance on third parties for AI technologies including Gen AI and large language models
risks: If third-party service providers fail to perform adequately or terminate relationships unexpectedly, sellers' ability to use platform to receive orders or payments could be adversely affected
Service outages from third-party providers could adversely impact payments made on platform
Third-party service providers may increase fees charged in the future
Inability to monitor or control third-party service providers' data security measures
Decision by third-party hosting providers to close facilities could cause system interruptions and delays
Shipping and postal rate increases may reduce competitiveness of sellers' offerings
Postal service changes and disruptions could require sellers to utilize more expensive alternatives
Problems with or price increases by third parties who provide services could harm business

regulatory_risks: Payments reporting requirements for sellers in many jurisdictions including IRS Form 1099-K requirements in United States
EU Digital Services Act (DSA) imposing legal obligations on online marketplaces including verification of seller information, content moderation, notice obligations, advertising restrictions
UK Online Safety Act (OSA) creating requirements around monitoring and handling harmful content
UK Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act (DMCCA) expanding investigative and enforcement powers

General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in European Union imposing compliance obligations regarding handling of personal data with potential fines up to 20 million euros or 4% of annual worldwide revenue
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and other U.S. state privacy laws providing new privacy rights and obligations
European Union Artificial Intelligence Act (EU AI Act) with fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global turnover for non-compliance
California AI Transparency Act requiring AI-related disclosure requirements effective January 2026
Colorado AI Act effective February 2026
DAC 7 in European Union and Digital Sales Reporting Legislation (DSR) in United Kingdom imposing data reporting requirements
Sales and use tax, VAT, GST collection and remittance obligations in various jurisdictions
Internet sales tax legislation enacted in 45 U.S. states, District of Columbia and Puerto Rico
Export control and economic sanctions laws including U.S. Department of Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control and Department of Commerce's Bureau of Industry and Security
Antitrust and competition laws in U.S. and foreign jurisdictions
Regulations regarding auctions and handling of property by secondhand dealers or pawnbrokers in various U.S. states and foreign jurisdictions
European Union General Product Safety Regulation effective December 13, 2024
EU member country anti-waste regulations creating direct obligations on sellers
Clean Air Act, Toxic Substances Control Act and Federal Insecticide, Fungicide and Insecticide Act enforcement by EPA and DOJ
Controlled Substances Act compliance requirements
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard and rules governing electronic funds transfers
Communications-based laws for emails, texts and other communications
Intellectual property laws including copyright, trademark and patent laws
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and foreign laws prohibiting corrupt payments to government officials
Money laundering and terrorist financing prevention laws
Import and export requirements and restrictions including customs authorities' de minimis thresholds
Tariffs and trade policies including potential U.S. tariffs on goods from Canada, Mexico and China
Labor and employment laws including stringent local labor laws and regulations outside U.S.
Environmental packaging and waste and climate-related regulations
Accessibility laws and regulations
Real estate regulations
Telecommunications regulations
Advertising regulations
Health and safety regulations

sanction_risks: U.S. government sanctions and export controls prohibiting trade or financial transactions with certain countries, businesses, organizations and individuals
Sanctions imposed by Department of Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control
Export controls by Department of Commerce's Bureau of Industry and Security
Potential violations requiring divestment of certain businesses and assets and restricting ability to operate in certain jurisdictions
Sanctions screening requirements for payment processing
Risk of users violating sanctions policies
Potential fines, criminal actions or sanctions against company, officers or employees for violations
